evals.prompt: I need to upgrade my iOS BarcodeCapture app from SDK v6 to v7. Here is my current view controller: ViewControllerV6.swift.
evals.expected_output: The skill replaces DataCaptureContext(licenseKey:) with DataCaptureContext.initialize(licenseKey:) + DataCaptureContext.shared, replaces the manual CameraSettings + .auto pattern with BarcodeCapture.recommendedCameraSettings, informs the user about the SMART scan intention default change in v7, and provides the migration guide URL. Preserves the BarcodeCaptureListener implementation.

assertions.text: DataCaptureContext.initialize(licenseKey: is present in the output
DataCaptureContext(licenseKey: is NOT present in the output (deprecated v6 form removed)
CameraSettings() with preferredResolution = .auto is NOT present in the output
The user is informed about the new default SMART scan intention in v7
The migration guide URL (https://docs.scandit.com/sdks/ios/migrate-6-to-7/) is provided

evals.prompt: I'm upgrading my Scandit iOS barcode scanning SDK from v7 to v8. What do I need to change in my code?
evals.expected_output: The skill explains that native iOS BarcodeCapture has no breaking API changes in v7→v8. The only change to watch for is VideoResolution.auto deprecation. It does NOT tell the user to replace BarcodeCapture(context:settings:) with a different constructor pattern (that change is for cross-platform SDKs only).

assertions.text: The skill mentions that there are no breaking API changes for native iOS BarcodeCapture in v7→v8
The skill does NOT instruct the user to replace BarcodeCapture(context:settings:) with a different constructor + addMode pattern (that is a cross-platform SDK change)

evals.prompt: I want to upgrade my BarcodeCapture iOS integration to the latest Scandit SDK version. Here's my code and Package.resolved.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads Package.resolved to detect v6.28.1, determines the target is v8, applies both migrations (6→7 then 7→8) without asking the user for their version.

assertions.text: Summary mentions the detected version (6.28.1 or v6)
Did not ask the user for their version — proceeded autonomously
DataCaptureContext.initialize(licenseKey: is present (v6→v7 context update applied)
CameraSettings() with preferredResolution = .auto is NOT present
